name: Faun
description: Variable-character multimode filter with continuously variable slopes across lowpass, bandpass, notch, and highpass. A Timbre circuit adds passband ripple for vocal or liquid colour, plus two inputs, V/Oct, and attenuverted FM.

controls.name: Cutoff
controls.description: Sets the cutoff frequency for all four simultaneous outputs.

controls.name: Slope
controls.description: Continuously varies the slope of each output, sweeping lowpass and highpass from -6 dB to -24 dB and tilting the bandpass and notch shapes.

controls.name: Timbre
controls.description: Introduces ripple in the passband (like adding a comb filter) without changing filter type or slope.

controls.name: Input 2 Gain
controls.description: Sets the level of the variable-gain input, from attenuation up to 4x gain for feedback patches and drive.

controls.name: FM Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales and inverts the FM CV applied to cutoff.

inputs.name: Audio In 2
inputs.description: Variable-gain audio input with up to 4x gain, useful for feedback patching and extra drive.

inputs.name: V/Oct
inputs.description: Pitch-tracking input for the cutoff frequency.

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: Frequency modulation input for the cutoff, scaled by the attenuverter.

outputs.name: Lowpass
outputs.description: Lowpass output, slope continuously variable from -6 dB to -24 dB.

outputs.name: Bandpass
outputs.description: Bandpass output whose shape tilts between -18 dB LP / -6 dB HP and -6 dB LP / -18 dB HP flanks.

outputs.name: Notch
outputs.description: Notch output whose shape tilts between -18 dB LP with notch and -18 dB HP with notch.

outputs.name: Highpass
outputs.description: Highpass output, slope continuously variable from -6 dB to -24 dB.

behaviors.name: Continuous slope morph
behaviors.description: One knob smoothly varies the curve of every output, so lowpass goes from gentle -6 dB tilt to aggressive -24 dB, and bandpass / notch shift their tilt balance rather than hard-switching modes.

behaviors.name: Passband ripple
behaviors.description: Timbre introduces comb-like ripple inside the passband without changing filter type or slope, moving the tone from clean to vocal, liquid, or formant-like.

behaviors.name: Variable input gain
behaviors.description: Input 2 provides up to 4x gain, so feeding the same source into In 1 and In 2 (or cross-patching an output back) can push the filter into louder, more coloured territory.

behaviors.name: Pitch tracking
behaviors.description: Cutoff tracks 1V/oct, so with high resonance or strong ripple the filter can be played as a pitched voice.

behaviors.name: Attenuverted FM
behaviors.description: The FM input passes through an attenuverter, so the same modulation source can sweep the cutoff up, down, or be dialled out entirely.

patchIdeas.name: Buttery sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Patch a saw VCO into Audio In 1 at unity, keep Timbre low, and sweep Slope slowly from gentle lowpass toward steep lowpass while an envelope opens Cutoff. The filter stays clean and precise while the curve changes under your hand.
steps: Patch VCO Saw out to Faun Audio In 1.
Patch an Envelope to Faun FM and set the attenuverter to about 2 o'clock (positive).
Start with Slope fully counter-clockwise and Timbre fully counter-clockwise.
Trigger the envelope, then slowly turn Slope clockwise while listening to the Lowpass output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Lowpass output: a saw that gets progressively darker as the envelope falls, with a smoothly steepening slope.
patchIdeas.hear: A soft, buttery downward sweep that tightens into a more focused lowpass the further right you turn Slope.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Cutoff: noon
* Slope: counter-clockwise to noon
* Timbre: fully counter-clockwise
* FM atv: 2 o'clock
* Input 2 Gain: off

VCO:
Envelope:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> Faun (Audio In 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> Faun (FM)
- Faun (Lowpass) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Liquid formant bandpass
patchIdeas.patch: Push Timbre up so the passband develops ripple, then send a slow LFO into FM with the attenuverter around 11 o'clock. The bandpass output moves between vocal and liquid formant shapes while base pitch stays centred.
steps: Patch VCO to Faun Audio In 1.
Patch a slow triangle LFO to Faun FM and set the attenuverter to about 11 o'clock.
Set Cutoff to noon, Slope to noon, Timbre to 2 o'clock.
Monitor the Bandpass output and fine-tune Timbre until the tone sounds vocal rather than whistly.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Bandpass output: a band of the VCO that drifts up and down with the LFO, with comb-like ripple colouring the passband.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow, liquid 'ooh / aah' motion, like a soft formant being swept across the VCO without the pitch changing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Cutoff: noon
* Slope: noon
* Timbre: 2 o'clock
* FM atv: 11 o'clock
* Input 2 Gain: off

VCO:
LFO:
Output:

- VCO (Out) -> Faun (Audio In 1)
- LFO (Tri) >> Faun (FM)
- Faun (Bandpass) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Feedback drive
patchIdeas.patch: Drive Audio In 2 with the same VCO plus a touch of feedback from the lowpass output. With Input 2 Gain up and Timbre high, the filter stops sounding polite and starts to bite without relying on external distortion.
steps: Multiple the VCO: one copy to Audio In 1 at unity, one copy to Audio In 2.
Patch the Lowpass output through a passive multiple: one tap to your mixer, one tap back into Audio In 2 (or mix it in with a small mixer) for feedback.
Raise Input 2 Gain past noon, set Cutoff around 11 o'clock, Slope around 1 o'clock, Timbre high.
Play a note and nudge Input 2 Gain and Timbre together until the filter just starts to growl.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Lowpass output: a heavily coloured, saturated lowpass with bite on transients.
patchIdeas.hear: A crunchier, more acidic version of the VCO that reacts strongly to Cutoff movement; back off Input 2 Gain to clean it up.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Cutoff: 11 o'clock
* Slope: 1 o'clock
* Timbre: 2 o'clock
* Input 2 Gain: past noon
* FM atv: off

VCO:
MultA:
MultB:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> MultA (In)
- MultA (Out 1) -> Faun (Audio In 1)
- MultA (Out 2) -> Faun (Audio In 2)
- Faun (Lowpass) -> MultB (In)
- MultB (Out 1) -> Output (In)
- MultB (Out 2) -> Faun (Audio In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Pitched notch voice
patchIdeas.patch: Play V/Oct from a sequencer with high Timbre and listen to the Notch output. The filter sits between effect and pitched source, especially with light FM adding movement around each step.
steps: Patch a pink-noise or VCO source into Faun Audio In 1.
Patch the sequencer pitch CV into Faun V/Oct.
Patch a slow LFO into Faun FM with the attenuverter around 10 o'clock.
Set Slope toward highpass, Timbre around 1 o'clock, Cutoff around noon.
Take the Notch output to your mixer and run the sequence; tweak Timbre for how 'pitched' the notch should sound.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Notch output: the source with a swept notch that tracks each sequencer step, plus slow drift from the LFO via FM.
patchIdeas.hear: A hollow, whistling line that follows the sequence like a melody played by a filter rather than a VCO.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Faun:
* Cutoff: noon
* Slope: 2 o'clock
* Timbre: 1 o'clock
* Input 2 Gain: off
* FM atv: 10 o'clock

VCO:
Sequencer:
LFO:
Output:

- VCO (Out) -> Faun (Audio In 1)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Faun (V/Oct)
- LFO (Out) >> Faun (FM)
- Faun (Notch) -> Output (In)
